citations.claim: Darkbloom is the provider agent for Apple Silicon Macs with comprehensive architecture covering hardware detection, model management, backend lifecycle, coordinator connection, private text engine, attestation, and E2E crypto
CatalogModel struct defines the model metadata structure with fields for ID, display name, size, architecture, description, and memory requirements
The component is named 'darkbloom' version 0.4.7 with description 'EigenInference provider agent for Apple Silicon Macs' and builds a binary at src/main.rs
WebSocket client manages provider connection with automatic reconnection, registration, heartbeats, inference requests, attestation challenges, and result forwarding
Security module implements runtime protections including PT_DENY_ATTACH, SIP verification, and memory wiping to prevent inspection of inference data
Backend management supports vllm-mlx and mlx-lm with health monitoring, automatic restart, and BackendManager wrapper for lifecycle management
Hardware detection queries macOS system APIs for memory size, CPU cores, machine model, and GPU capabilities using sysctl and system_profiler
NodeKeyPair implements ephemeral X25519 key pairs for E2E encryption, generated fresh on each launch and protected by PT_DENY_ATTACH + Hardened Runtime + SIP
Model scanning examines HuggingFace cache for MLX models, filtering by available memory with detection heuristics for directory names containing 'mlx' and quantization hints
Hypervisor integration uses Apple's framework to create lightweight VM with Stage 2 page tables for memory isolation, requiring 16MB alignment and pre-allocated pools
Request proxy handles legacy/local flows between coordinator WebSocket and local backends, with E2E decryption happening before proxy handling
Service management provides launchd user agent integration with explicit user control - only runs when started via 'darkbloom start' or app toggle, no auto-start on login
Telemetry architecture uses multiple sources (tracing layer, panic hook, backend scraper, direct emit) feeding into mpsc queue, async batcher, and HTTPS POST to coordinator with disk overflow

Dependencies include tokio for async runtime, reqwest for HTTP, tokio-tungstenite for WebSocket, axum for HTTP server, serde for serialization, crypto_box for NaCl encryption, and security-framework for macOS integration
CLI interface provides comprehensive subcommands including init, serve, install, models, earnings, doctor, start/stop, logs, update, login/logout, and autoupdate configuration
Build-time coordinator URLs default to production (api.darkbloom.dev) with R2 CDN URLs for model and site-packages distribution, configurable via environment variables
Build system integrates Swift Secure Enclave library, linking EigenInferenceEnclave static library with Foundation, Security, and CryptoKit frameworks plus Swift runtime paths

Wire protocol defines JSON-encoded WebSocket messages with provider-to-coordinator messages (Register, Heartbeat, InferenceResponse, AttestationResponse) and coordinator-to-provider messages (InferenceRequest, Cancel, AttestationChallenge)
Service startup includes single-instance enforcement via PID file, process cleanup, hypervisor VM creation, security posture verification, and core dump disable before processing any inference data
Coordinator connection is deliberately delayed until all backends are loaded to prevent routing requests before the provider can serve them, ensuring service readiness
